title: Decolonization scorecard for clinical trial portfolios across 15 African nations
primary_estimand: Five-dimension Decolonization Score (0-100) measuring local sponsorship, Phase 1 sovereignty, burden-research alignment, institutional diversity, and per-capita trial density
certainty: high (complete registry enumeration with structured scoring rubric)
body: Can registry data be used to quantify research sovereignty across African clinical trial portfolios? We scored 15 African countries on five prespecified dimensions derived from published decolonization frameworks: local sponsorship, phase 1 sovereignty, burden-research alignment, institutional diversity, and per-capita trial density, each contributing 0 to 20 points to a composite 0 to 100 Decolonization Score. All interventional trials were retrieved from ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, with sponsor origin classified by country-specific keywords and condition focus compared against WHO burden rankings. South Africa and Egypt scored highest overall, whereas DRC, Burkina Faso, and Senegal scored lowest because local sponsorship and phase 1 capacity were sparse. The continental average was below 50, indicating that foreign-led portfolios remain dominant in most countries. Phase 1 sovereignty was the weakest dimension across the set, highlighting a gap at the earliest stage of therapeutic development. The score is limited by registry coverage, keyword-based sponsor classification, and the assumptions built into the scoring rubric.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.
